chapters.title: Every designer starts somewhere...
chapters.description: This is my journey of evolution. Three years of turning curiosity into code.

chapters.title: Year 1: The Foundations
chapters.description: I started with a blank screen and a lot of questions. This year was about mastering the languages of the web: HTML and CSS.
chapters.reflection: Looking back, my code was messy, but my passion was high. I learned that structure is everything—without a solid foundation, a beautiful design will eventually fall apart.

chapters.title: Year 2: The Logic Leap
chapters.description: Things started clicking. I moved beyond static pages and dove into the world of JavaScript and interactivity.
chapters.reflection: This was the 'frustration year.' Learning logic taught me that coding is 90% problem-solving and 10% typing. I moved from making things look good to making things work.

chapters.title: Year 3: The Polished Professional
chapters.description: Now, I focus on user experience, performance, and clean, modern aesthetics.
chapters.reflection: I no longer just build websites; I build experiences. My focus has shifted to the user—how they feel, how they move, and how I can make their journey seamless.

chapters.title: The Next Chapter
chapters.description: My journey doesn't end here. I'm ready to take these skills to the next level.
